allelopathic.label: Allelopathic
allelopathic.explanation: One plant releases chemical compounds (through roots, leaves, or decomposing residue) that inhibit the other's growth or seed germination nearby.

nutrient_competition.label: Nutrient competition
nutrient_competition.explanation: Both plants draw heavily on the same soil nutrients, so planted close together they'll compete and both may come up shorted.

pest_crossover.label: Pest crossover
pest_crossover.explanation: Both plants attract the same pest species, so growing them near each other makes it easier for an infestation on one to spread to the other.

disease_shared.label: Shared disease risk
disease_shared.explanation: Both plants are vulnerable to the same disease, so close planting raises the chance an outbreak on one spreads to the other.

space_competition.label: Space competition
space_competition.explanation: Both plants compete aggressively for the same light, root space, or airflow, so crowding them together stunts one or both.
